change_request: Add FAQPage JSON-LD schema to /en/faq page via buildFaqPageSchema exported from lib/faq.ts, with unit tests
acceptance_criteria: buildFaqPageSchema exported from lib/faq.ts returns null for non-English and FAQPage schema for English
schema mainEntity has 7 Question entries each with acceptedAnswer
schema content matches getFAQItems en output
faq page component uses buildFaqPageSchema and renders script block only for English

scope.change_request: Add FAQPage JSON-LD schema to /en/faq page via buildFaqPageSchema exported from lib/faq.ts, with unit tests
acceptance_criteria: buildFaqPageSchema exported from lib/faq.ts returns null for non-English and FAQPage schema for English
schema mainEntity has 7 Question entries each with acceptedAnswer
schema content matches getFAQItems en output
faq page component uses buildFaqPageSchema and renders script block only for English

llm_analysis.assessment: The change aligns with the request: it adds FAQPage JSON-LD generation in lib/faq.ts, wires it into the /[lang]/faq page, and adds unit tests covering English-only behavior and schema content.
concerns: The page now emits a raw <script> tag via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without explicit escaping or a helper component, which is acceptable for JSON-LD but should be reviewed for serialization safety.

The implementation relies on buildFaqPageSchema(lang) returning null for non-English, but the page still calls it for all languages rather than gating earlier; this is fine functionally but worth noting.

risks: app/[lang]/faq/page.tsx:FaqPage may render invalid or duplicated structured data if the page is ever server-rendered alongside another FAQ JSON-LD source.
lib/faq.ts:buildFaqPageSchema uses a broad string input and only checks for 'en'; unexpected locale formats like 'en-US' will return null and suppress schema unexpectedly.
app/[lang]/faq/page.tsx: the script block is inserted before the visible content, which could affect ordering assumptions in tests or downstream DOM consumers.
lib/faq.test.ts: tests validate current FAQ_ITEMS shape but do not assert that the schema is JSON-serializable or that the script output is present only on the English page component.

llm_analysis.notes: The schema builder accepts a string locale and only handles the exact 'en' case. There is no explicit handling for null/undefined/malformed locale values, though the current call site passes a validated lang value.

affected_apis: app/[lang]/faq/page.tsx: FAQ page server component output now includes application/ld+json script for English
JSON-LD FAQPage schema shape: @type=FAQPage with mainEntity Question/Answer entries
llm_analysis.notes: This is an additive change to page output and a new exported helper. No existing public API appears to be removed or renamed.

llm_analysis.recommendation: Run the full test suite and verify the rendered /en/faq HTML contains exactly one FAQPage JSON-LD script and that non-English pages do not emit it.

rollback.head: 121789dcfc56f890b901a5e4280f10851bdb2cc9
rollback_steps: 2. Resolve any merge conflicts if prompted

overall.summary: The implementation satisfies the requested behavior and the tests cover the key acceptance criteria: English-only schema generation, seven FAQ entries, and content parity with getFAQItems('en'). The page integration is straightforward and low risk, with only minor concerns around locale exact-match behavior and JSON-LD script serialization.
key_concerns: Exact locale matching may suppress schema for locale variants like en-US if those ever reach this helper.
No explicit test asserts the page component renders the script only for English, though the helper tests make that behavior likely.
